item.title: Tour de France 2026: Pedersen praised for team effort as Træen takes yellow on stage 4
item.description: Tour riders are speaking out about the extreme heat, but is anyone listening? Mads Pedersen answered his critics with a strong team performance, while Torstein Træen was left in disbelief after taking the yellow jersey. The jury handed out two penalties on stage 4, and Pogacar lost yellow but gained time overall. Stage 5 preview: the first sprint chance has arrived. Hushovd called it a proud moment as Træen delivered a historic yellow jersey for Uno-X. O'Brien was the first rider out of time on stage 4. Quinn Simmons said he told the team to bring him, and he justified his selection with a key assist. The standings changed as Træen took yellow and Pedersen moved into green. Lidl-Trek also explained why the tougher Tour de France stages matter for Pedersen's green jersey bid.

item.title: Tour de France stage 5 preview: Philipsen could be the sprint pick
item.description: It is very hot in France, and the heat is affecting the Tour de France. On stage 4, the UCI allowed riders to receive extra bottles in feed zones so they could cool down more often and more effectively. More heat is expected on Wednesday, which could make Jasper Philipsen a strong option for stage 5.

item.title: Tour 2026: Kelland O’Brien misses time limit and exits the race
item.description: Kelland O'Brien is the third rider to abandon the Tour de France 2026. O'Brien had already been dropped early in the stage.

item.title: Tour de France Daily #4: Mads Pedersen and Lidl-Trek dominate in Foix (Podcast)
item.description: A perfect day for Mads Pedersen and Lidl-Trek. The Dane made it into the big breakaway that shaped stage 4 of the Tour de France 2026 and finished it off in style, with strong support from Quinn Simmons and an tireless Matthias Vacek. The general classification also changed, with Torsten Traen moving into the lead thanks to the time gained on the peloton. It was perhaps a predictable finish, but the stage still delivered plenty of excitement. We discuss all this and more in our Tour de France Daily on SpazioTalk.

item.title: “We didn’t expect to be this good. The team mattered more than ego,” says Vacek after Tour stage four
item.description: Lidl-Trek put three riders in the long break on stage four of the Tour de France, and each had a chance to win, including Czech champion Mathias Vacek. The 24-year-old was key for Mads Pedersen, helping him over the hardest climbs, covering attacks, and leading out the sprint. Vacek also got a reward of his own, taking the white jersey podium.

item.title: Pedersen praised Vacek and Simmons’ work and dedicated the win to the departing Lidl-Trek boss
item.description: Mads Pedersen claimed his first victory of the season by winning stage four of the Tour de France after excellent teamwork with Mathias Vacek and Quinn Simmons. The Danish star admitted he could not have done it without them.

item.title: Vacek helped Pedersen win Tour de France stage five and took the white jersey himself
item.description: Mathias Vacek was the strongest rider in stage four of the Tour de France to Foix, and Mads Pedersen would not have won without his help. The Czech champion could have won the stage from the break himself, but he worked for his leader and it paid off. Pedersen is the new points classification leader, while Vacek moved up to third overall and first in the young rider classification.

item.title: Podcast - BONUS: CykloPodcast x Velo Fokus after stage 4 of the Tour de France
item.description: An unexpected breakaway success became reality. A few years ago, this stage would have been a clear breakaway stage, but in today's Grand Tour peloton dynamics nothing is guaranteed. Even Lidl – Trek had no win in hand despite its high quality. The reality of the day was the numerical and classic-rider strength of the team around Mads Pedersen, who beat the rest of the surviving breakaway in the sprint. Torstein Træen capped a successful day for Scandinavia by taking the yellow jersey.

item.title: Podcast - BONUS: CykloPodcast x Velo Fokus after stage 3 of the Tour de France
item.description: The Tour began in style, and UAE decided not to give away anything early on. The opening team time trial only delayed the winning mood in the team of defending champion Tadej Pogačar. Isaac del Toro impressed on his debut in Barcelona. In stage 3, when it looked like someone from the breakaway might take the win, Pogačar's teammates accelerated and the top favorite took both the stage and the yellow jersey.

item.title: Boonen criticizes Lotto-Intermarché for starting Arnaud De Lie: 'It makes the problem even bigger'
item.description: Arnaud De Lie's Tour de France ended after just three stages. The Lotto-Intermarché rider was already ill before the start and tried unsuccessfully to get through the opening stages. The decision to include him in the Tour de France lineup has drawn heavy criticism for Lotto-Intermarché.

item.title: RAD-MARATHON Tannheimer Tal sets new entry record

item.title: Jai Hindley stays loyal to Red Bull-BORA-hansgrohe despite interest from several teams
item.description: Jai Hindley will remain with Red Bull-BORA-hansgrohe for the coming years. The 30-year-old Australian finished a strong third in the Giro d'Italia 2026 behind Jonas Vingegaard and Felix Gall. He has previously finished second and first in the Giro d'Italia. He is currently riding as a domestique for Remco Evenepoel.

item.title: LIVE Tour de France 2026, Stage 4
item.description: Welcome to the full interactive live text coverage of the fourth stage of the Tour de France 2026. After three days of intense racing, today could be the first chance for the general classification riders to take a breath, as much as possible. It is 181.9 kilometers from Carcassonne to Foix on a day that looks made for the breakaway. Expect attacks from the start, with many riders trying to get away and take advantage of a day when the peloton may allow the move to go. And who knows whether Tadej Pogačar will decide to put the yellow jersey on the line...

item.title: Heat leaves Tour organiser with headaches as cancellation is no longer unthinkable
item.description: Temperatures of around 39°C are forecast in Carcassonne, the start town of stage 4 in this Tour. And that is before the riders face more tropical conditions in the coming days. Tour organiser ASO admits the heat is causing major concern. Possible solutions include shortening stages, starting earlier or neutralising them. Cancellation is the last resort.

item.title: Ill Uijtdebroeks tries to survive the first stages of the Tour de France; De Lie abandons with infection in stage 3
item.description: After the first three stages, Cian Uijtdebroeks, leader of Movistar, has lost nearly three and a half minutes. The young Belgian knows why he is struggling: he is ill. According to the Spanish team, Uijtdebroeks will try to continue in the race under medical supervision. Arnaud De Lie has already left the Tour de France after failing to finish stage 3 because of an intestinal infection.

item.title: Missing from the Tour, six WorldTour teams head to Austria, giving ATT Investments and Kasper crypto4me a chance to shine
item.description: The Tour de France is now in full swing. Riders from six WorldTour teams who were not selected for the biggest race in the world will compete in the Tour of Austria from Wednesday to Sunday. The two best Czech continental teams will also be in the peloton. Last year, Piotr Pekala of ATT Investments was one of only two continental riders to finish in the top 12 in the GC. He, along with teammate Albert Monti and Václav Ježek of Kasper crypto4me, should be the main hopes for the Czech teams again this year.

item.title: Tour de France 2026 roundup: Pidcock suffers in the heat, Pogacar takes yellow, De Lie abandons
item.description: Tour de France 2026 stage 3 brought a brutal day in the heat for Tom Pidcock, while Tadej Pogacar’s fast start forced Visma to play the long game. Arnaud De Lie abandoned after saying he simply did not have the legs, Jonas Vingegaard reacted to losing yellow, and Remco Evenepoel explained why his punch was missing. The jury issued eight penalties, Paul Seixas passed his first Pyrenean test, and Pogacar took yellow and green as Baudin moved into the polka dots. Stage 4 preview: will the breakaway finally get its chance?

item.title: Tour de France Daily #3: Tadej Pogačar takes the spotlight and the yellow jersey (Podcast)
item.description: Tour de France Daily returns with a special episode from SpazioTalk to discuss stage 3 of the Tour de France. The race is back in France, and Tadej Pogačar immediately took center stage. After letting his teammate Isaac Del Toro take the win in Barcelona, the Slovenian dominated in Les Angles, completing the classic double: stage win and yellow jersey. The gaps in the general classification remain small, and tomorrow could already see the lead challenged on a day that looks favorable for breakaways. Listen to the full episode below.

item.title: Wout Poels puts up a strong fight, but Finn takes second stage win at the Sibiu Cycling Tour
item.description: Wout Poels came close to his first win for Unibet Rose Rockets at the Sibiu Cycling Tour. In the queen stage to Bâlea Lac, the 38-year-old Dutchman was only beaten by Lorenzo Finn. The Italian talent took his second straight stage win and remains in the lead overall.

item.title: Sibiu Tour 2026: Lorenzo Finn also conquers the Bâlea Lac climb and strengthens his lead
item.description: Lorenzo Finn made it two wins at the Sibiu Tour 2026. After taking his first professional victory on the uphill finish in Păltiniș, the Red Bull-Bora-hansgrohe talent repeated the feat at the summit finish in Bâlea Lac, strengthening his lead in the general classification. More information, results and standings for stage 3 of the Sibiu Tour 2026 will follow.

item.title: Pogacar turns Del Toro’s Tour win into a Mexican celebration
item.description: Video: Pogacar turns Del Toro’s Tour win into a Mexican celebration. Analysis: Tadej Pogacar’s early gift sends an ominous message to Tour de France rivals. Matxín said Pogacar produced a masterpiece after UAE’s 1-2. Race preview: Tour de France stage 3 could be another GC day or a chance for the breakaway. Seixas stayed on course despite a radio mix-up in a tense Tour finale. A wildfire forced a spectator ban for the final 40km of Tour de France stage 3. Evenepoel said he may have won if he had taken the final corner differently on Montjuïc. Visma were satisfied as Vingegaard defended yellow in a tricky Tour finale. Del Toro said he was a very privileged guy after opening his Tour account with Pogacar’s blessing. Tour de France 2026 standings: Vingegaard defended yellow, while Del Toro took green and white. Race report: Del Toro mastered Montjuïc with Pogacar’s help to win Tour de France stage 2. Tour de France 2026 jury report: Three penalties were handed out on stage 2.

item.description: Jonas Vingegaard responded calmly to UAE Emirates-XRG’s display of strength on stage 2 of the Tour de France. The Dane was able to follow Isaac del Toro and Tadej Pogacar, but while Vingegaard was at his limit, Pogacar controlled the rest and gave Del Toro the stage win. Vingegaard kept the yellow jersey.

item.title: Tour de France 2026, Remco Evenepoel 3rd: 'I'm happy with the sensations, I think it's positive to start with good results'
item.description: Remco Evenepoel was beaten only by UAE Team Emirates XRG in the second stage of the Tour de France 2026. The Olympic champion finished third on the Barcelona finish line behind Isaac Del Toro and Tadej Pogačar, who arrived almost together. Evenepoel tried to close the gap in the finale, which allowed him to move ahead of Jonas Vingegaard (Visma | Lease a Bike) and gain 4 seconds on him in the general classification, moving up to third overall at 15 seconds behind the Dane.

item.title: UAE manager names the team's standout rider after Tour de France 1-2, and it is neither Pogacar nor Del Toro
item.description: UAE Emirates-XRG showed its strength on Sunday in stage two of the Tour de France. Isaac del Toro and Tadej Pogacar took a clear 1-2 on Montjuic, responding after Visma | Lease a Bike won the team time trial on Saturday.

item.title: Stage 3 favourites (Granollers-Les Angles): will the GC riders leave the win and yellow jersey to the breakaway?
item.description: Will the general classification riders target this stage, which is more tricky than hard, or will they leave the win and the yellow jersey to the breakaway? If so, many riders could contend for both. One condition: survive the first-category climb about 78 kilometres from the finish. After that comes a tough finale and a battle for the stage win on a lighter version of the Mur de Bretagne. Wildfires in the region could still lead to route changes.

item.title: Alex Molenaar wants to defend the polka dot jersey: “I hope they put polka dots on my bike, shorts and helmet”
item.description: A great start to the Tour de France for debutant Alex Molenaar. The Dutch-Spanish rider took the polka dot jersey from Tadej Pogacar on the second day. Before the stage, that was not necessarily his plan, he said afterwards on camera. “It was the team’s plan, but…”

item.title: Final section of Tour de France 2026 stage 3 closed to spectators
item.description: French authorities have decided that the part of stage 3 on French territory must take place without spectators. The prefecture of Pyrénées-Orientales announced this on Sunday evening via AFP. Wildfires about 70 kilometres from the finish in Les Angles are the reason for the decision.

item.title: Remco Evenepoel deliberately left a gap to Del Toro: “I hoped they would slow down”
item.description: Remco Evenepoel had mixed feelings after the second stage of the Tour de France. The Belgian rode well, but missed out on the stage win, partly because he deliberately left a gap to stage winner Isaac Del Toro on the way to the final climb, which cost him some energy before the sprint.

No spectators allowed on French territory for Tour's third stage due to wildfires
item.description: The third stage of the Tour de France will go ahead on Monday, but in a modified form. No spectators will be allowed either. The Pyrénées-Orientales prefecture announced the measures on Sunday evening. They are the result of the severe wildfires currently burning about 70 kilometres from the finish in Les Angles. The fires have already destroyed around 1,500 hectares.

item.title: Tour de France 2026, all withdrawals
item.description: The Tour de France 2026 peloton lasted just one day. The first withdrawal from the Grande Boucle came on the evening of day one, although it was officially recorded on the second day of racing, as Clément Berthet (Groupama-FDJ United) had managed to finish the team time trial despite a heavy crash. The incident, however, left him with significant consequences and made it impossible to continue such a demanding race. The French rider is therefore the first rider to abandon this edition, and did not start stage 2. Tour de France 2026 withdrawals Stage 1 – Stage 2 Clément Berthet (Groupama-FDJ United)

item.title: Pinarello Q36.5 Cycling Team, Damien Howson announces retirement at the end of the season
item.description: Damien Howson will retire from professional cycling at the end of the 2026 season, ending a career that began in the summer of 2013 as a stagiaire with Orica GreenEDGE. Born in 1992, the Australian first made his mark internationally by winning the under-23 time trial at the 2013 World Championships in Florence. He then spent many years with GreenEDGE, under various names, becoming a reliable domestique and picking up some strong results in smaller stage races. He joined the Q36.5 project in 2023.

item.title: Manhole cover causes XDS Astana crash in team time trial
item.description: XDS Astana lost 2:18 in the Tour de France team time trial, an unusually large gap for a 20-kilometre effort. The cause was unclear during the broadcast, but it later emerged on X that three riders crashed hard after hitting a manhole cover. Groupama-FDJ United also had a crash.

item.title: Tour 2026: Clément Berthet first rider to abandon after heavy team time trial crash
item.description: The first rider to abandon the Tour de France 2026 is already known. Clément Berthet of Groupama-FDJ United was forced out after 20 kilometres when he crashed in the team time trial through Barcelona after losing control on a slightly raised manhole cover.

item.title: Madness or genius? How a bold tactical plan put Jonas Vingegaard in yellow
item.description: It seemed to go against the basic rules of a team time trial: the best three riders doing almost no work for most of the race. Madness or genius? The latter. After Visma-Lease a Bike won the team time trial, Jonas Vingegaard is back in yellow for the first time in three years. It came thanks to a big gamble, an unknown British former rider, and trust in the computer.

Tour de France 2026 route preview and stage 2 favorites: Tarragona
item.description: After the opening team time trial, the Tour de France 2026 gets its first road stage. On Sunday 5 July, the Tarragona-Barcelona stage will be the second and final stage in Spain in this edition of the Tour. Unlike in the past, the sprinters will have to wait longer for a real chance at stage victory. The final circuit in Barcelona includes the tough climb to Montjuic Castle, making the stage suitable for Classics riders and stage hunters. The general classification leaders will also need to be ready on stage 2.

item.title: Tour de France 2026, Jonas Vingegaard takes back the Yellow Jersey: “It’s still a long way to go, but it’s the perfect start”
item.description: Jonas Vingegaard is back in the Yellow Jersey at the Tour de France after three years. The last time the Dane wore the symbol of the lead at the Grande Boucle was in Paris, in the 2023 edition he won. Since then, a long chase that ended today in Barcelona, at the end of the opening team time trial. Team Visma | Lease a Bike won the stage, dominating the course with perfect tactical management, and the Giro d’Italia winner has already gained 12 seconds on rival Tadej Pogačar. It may seem like nothing, given that …

item.title: Juan Ayuso finishes exhausted after chasing the yellow jersey at the Tour

item.description: The second half-stage of the opening day at the Sibiu Tour 2026 ends with a win for Julius Johansen. The UAE Team Emirates – XRG rider dominated the short time trial, covering the 3.2 kilometers in 4:16. He beat Portuguese rider Ivo Oliveira, his teammate, by just two seconds. Michiel Mouris (Red Bull – BORA hansgrohe) completed the podium, six seconds down. Manuel Oioli (Swatt Club) narrowly missed the top 10, finishing 11th at 11 seconds behind the winner. Thanks to this victory, Johansen moves into the overall lead, taking over from the ...

item.title: These GC contenders can already be ruled out after the team time trial at the Tour de France
item.description: A team time trial on day one of a Grand Tour does not usually tell the whole story, but the new format at the Tour de France still allowed several conclusions after stage 1. Because the fastest rider’s time counted for the team, some GC contenders can already be ruled out after losing time.

item.title: Tour de France 2026, Cian Uijtdebroeks hit by cramps in the team time trial: “I did what I could”
item.description: Cian Uijtdebroeks got off to a difficult start at the Tour de France 2026. The Belgian arrived with high hopes for his first Grande Boucle, after not previously finding a place in his former teams. His winter move to Movistar gave him the team’s backing, and the Spanish squad was hoping for a strong performance on home roads in the Barcelona team time trial. Things went well until the third intermediate check, when the Belgian leader began to show signs of fatigue and cramps.

item.title: Tour 2026: Belgian GC hopeful loses time in the team time trial

item.title: RoadCast #10: The Czech and Slovak championships in Jeseník, the Tour de France, and why Mathias Vacek could shine
item.description: The Czech and Slovak championships in Jeseník are over, and the Tour de France is about to begin. In episode 10 of the RoadCast podcast, we review the national championships, break down the races and the performances of the Czech riders, and then focus on the biggest race of the season. We also discuss the start in Barcelona, the battle between Tadej Pogacar and Jonas Vingegaard, the chances of Mathias Vacek, Pavel Bittner, and Jakub Otruba, and our picks for the biggest surprise of this year's Tour.

item.title: Who will win the green jersey and who will be the best climber at the 113th Tour de France?
item.description: In the two points competitions at this year's Tour de France, we should see battles between different types of riders. In the points classification, it will be between the fastest sprinters and aggressive riders such as Mads Pedersen, while in the mountains classification, breakaway riders will try to beat the best climbers among the overall contenders.

item.title: Pogačar satisfied after third place in the team time trial
item.description: Tadej Pogačar said he was very happy with his first test at this year's Tour de France. The defending champion lost 12 seconds to Jonas Vingegaard on the opening day, but said he was simply glad to have such a tough day behind him.

item.title: Vingegaard calls Tour de France start perfect after Visma-Lease a Bike team time trial win
item.description: Jonas Vingegaard returned to the top of the general classification at the Tour de France after almost three years, in an emotional moment for him. Visma-Lease a Bike brought their leader to the final climb, where he rewarded the team with the fastest time and the stage win. The riders were delighted, but Vingegaard said the Tour has only just begun.

item.title: Vingegaard takes yellow as Visma-Lease a Bike wins opening team time trial
item.description: Visma-Lease a Bike won the opening team time trial of the Tour de France in Barcelona, and Jonas Vingegaard became the first rider to wear the yellow jersey. The second favorite for the overall title beat Netcompany Ineos by 7 seconds and UAE Emirates-XRG by 12 seconds. That same gap now separates Vingegaard and Tadej Pogačar after stage 1.

item.title: Boguslawski wins overall as ATT Investments take three stage victories and two more podiums at Solidarnosc
item.description: The five-stage Course Cycliste de Solidarnosc et des Champions Olympiques (2.2) is over. Marcel Boguslawski won three stages, and ATT Investments riders finished second in the other two, giving the Polish sprinter the overall victory. Add in other strong results and, above all, 86 UCI points, and it was an outstanding race for the Pardubice team.

item.title: Maryland Cycling Classic 2026 cancelled, organizers aim to return in 2027
item.description: The Maryland Cycling Classic 2026 will not take place on September 6. In a statement, the organizers announced the cancellation of the US race but did not give a reason. Cyclingnews reported that funding problems had emerged in recent months. Created in 2020 and first postponed to 2021, then to 2022 because of the Covid-19 pandemic, the race was also cancelled in 2024 after the Baltimore bridge collapse. So far, it has held three men's editions (2022, 2023 and 2025) and one women's edition (2025).

item.title: Specialized S-Works Tarmac SL8 gets up to 16% off as SL9 grabs Tour headlines
item.description: It looks almost identical and weighs nearly the same, and unless you're Remco Evenepoel you may not notice a performance difference. With savings of up to £1,150, the Tarmac SL8 has become a strong value buy.

item.title: “It really does change your bearings”: mixed views on the new team time trial format that opens the Tour de France
item.description: Individual times will be taken in the team time trial this Saturday in Barcelona, a first in the Tour that changes how riders approach this special event.

item.title: “If Pogi eats a sandwich and a banana, everyone starts eating them too”: Ion Izagirre reflects on the peloton
item.description: With retirement approaching, 37-year-old Ion Izagirre, a pro since 2011 and a two-time stage winner at the Tour de France, looks back on how cycling has changed ahead of his final Tour de France with Cofidis.

item.title: Tadej Pogacar’s races are decided in the mind too: big performances, winning obsession and mental health
item.description: By hiding his knee injury at last year’s Tour de France, Tadej Pogacar increased the pressure on himself and later admitted he struggled mentally. But according to those close to him, he never fully broke down.

item.title: Two wins in three days and a comfortable leader: UAE and Tadej Pogacar are not leaving anything behind at this Tour de France
item.description: By taking a second win at this Tour de France on Monday, with leader Tadej Pogacar back in the yellow jersey, UAE have no intention of leaving anything to the other teams.

item.title: Remco Evenepoel on Pogacar, the Tour de France, and the Book of Questions
item.description: Remco Evenepoel, 26, knows what awaits him in the Tour de France: 21 stages, 3,333 kilometers, and 54,450 meters of climbing. He also answers five questions from the Book of Questions, including his view on Tadej Pogacar and the doubts from outside the sport.

item.title: Remco Evenepoel: “Pogacar flying past us in training? Maxim Van Gils exaggerated a bit”
item.description: Remco Evenepoel (26) knows what awaits him in the Tour de France: 21 stages, 3,333 kilometers, and 54,450 meters of climbing. So five pressing questions from the Book of Questions are no problem either. “Without Pogacar, I would now have two more Monuments and one more world title. That removes the outside doubt.”

item.title: Stage 1 favorites (Barcelona-Barcelona): taking yellow will be a very tough task for Remco Evenepoel
item.description: The team time trial format adds an interesting twist, as the stage win will be decided by the time of the first rider from each team. Remco Evenepoel will certainly be eager for the yellow jersey, which would make him the 28th rider in history to have worn the leader’s jerseys in all three Grand Tours. But he will have to beat Netcompany INEOS, packed with time trial specialists, in-form UAE and Visma-Lease a Bike, which always performs well in this discipline.

item.title: Interview. Lucien Van Impe, the last Belgian Tour de France winner 50 years ago: “Evenepoel? I give another Belgian a better chance to succeed me”
item.description: Lucien Van Impe says: “If a new Belgian wins the Tour tomorrow, they will quickly forget me.” But we have now been waiting half a century for that new Belgian. It has been that long since Lucien Van Impe (79), the last Belgian winner of the Tour. Nieuwsblad has not forgotten. “Evenepoel my successor? I don't think so. If you don't have it, you don't have it.”

item.title: “Evenepoel my successor? If you don't have it, you don't have it”: 50 years ago Lucien Van Impe became the last Belgian to win the Tour

Video report: Jeseníky massacre, championship day with Fany Gastro Integray L27
item.description: Temperatures above 40 degrees, brutal pace from the start, and conditions that made it a matter of survival. Watch the immediate reactions from riders and coaches after the cycling championships in Jeseník, which went down in history as one of the toughest races ever. How did the Fany Gastro riders handle the hellish course? Who failed to finish within the time limit, and what are the plans for the upcoming U23 championships?

item.title: Podcast - Will we see a more balanced battle between Pogačar and Vingegaard?
item.description: The biggest event on the cycling calendar is almost here. The Tour de France, starting in Barcelona, promises another three-week showcase for the sport's elite. Jonas Vingegaard is chasing the Giro-Tour double, but standing in his way is defending champion and clear race favorite Tadej Pogačar. Based on his performances in Switzerland, the Slovenian looks a step above the rest of the general classification contenders, but 21 stages could still bring drama through heat, nerves, crashes, or withdrawals. Rising home talent Paul Seixas will make his Grand Tour debut. His form is uncertain after recently recovering from injuries sustained in a crash at the Tour Auvergne – Rhône-Alpes. The cycling summer and its main course of the season can begin.

Flat stages will now award far more points than before, perhaps to give sprinters a better chance at green, or mainly to curb Pogacar’s ambition.

item.title: SpazioTalk #189: the route of the Tour de France 2026
item.description: SpazioTalk returns with episode 189, an unusual midweek edition, to discuss the Tour de France 2026. After last episode’s analysis of the favourites, today we focus on the 21 stages that will shape the Grande Boucle. There are two time trials, one team and one individual, plus plenty of climbing through the Pyrenees, Massif Central, Vosges and Alps. The show promises plenty of action, and we highlight some key points that could decide the race, including the double Alpe d’Huez in the final stages.

item.title: Lidl-Trek to race a commemorative bike at the Tour de France 2026
item.description: The Tour de France 2026 will be special for Lidl-Trek. The team will celebrate 50 years of the bike maker’s history on the roads of the Grande Boucle and later at the Tour de France Femmes. For the occasion, Trek has prepared a unique frame that traces the company’s history since 1976, with the various brands, colors and symbols used over the years. The many colors will make the riders easy to spot in the bunch. The model will be called ICON 50. A responsibility, but also motivation for Mads Pedersen, ...

item.title: Ali Fielding announces retirement from Great Britain Cycling Team
item.description: Former British champion Ali Fielding has announced his retirement from the Great Britain Cycling Team after eight years in the sport. He played a key role in the men’s sprint squad and won medals at national, European and world level.

item.title: Junior and U23 track riders set for European Championships in Cottbus
item.description: The European Track Championships for junior and U23 riders will take place in Germany next week. On the partly covered track in Cottbus, near the Polish border, the Dutch team will mainly compete in the endurance events.

item.title: Provisional cyclocross calendar for 2026
item.description: The provisional cyclocross calendar for the 2026-2027 season has been announced. The first race is scheduled for the weekend of 26 September in Mechelen, while the first youth race is listed for 3 October in Norg.

item.title: Martinez will not target the general classification at the Tour
item.description: Last year he came close to winning the mountains classification, but in the end he had to accept that Tadej Pogačar and Jonas Vingegaard are simply out of reach in the current points system. Lenny Martinez returns to the Tour de France aiming to win the polka dot jersey, as he does not believe he can contend for the overall classification.

item.title: Seixas says he is in great shape ahead of Tour de France debut
item.description: He is likely the most closely watched debutant in this year's Tour de France. The 19-year-old Paul Seixas is expected to end France's more than 40-year wait for a home winner of the overall classification in the world's biggest race. After a strong spring, the Decathlon CMA CGM leader was among the three biggest favourites, but a crash and injury at Tour Auvergne–Rhône-Alpes lowered expectations. Seixas says there is no reason to worry, as he feels great.

item.title: Arroyomolinos presents the 2026 Spanish XCO-XCR Mountain Bike Championships
item.category: Real Federación Española de Ciclismo – RFEC
item.description: The Municipal Auditorium of Arroyomolinos hosted the presentation of the Spanish XCO-XCR Championships on Thursday. The event will bring together riders from July 17 to 19.

item.title: Eighty national titles awarded at the 2026 USA Cycling Masters Championships
item.description: More than 800 athletes from across the country gathered in Milwaukee from June 27 to July 1 to compete for national titles in the criterium, road race, and time trial.

item.title: ATT Investments complete a clean sweep at Solidarnosc as Marceli Boguslawski wins again
item.description: The third stage of the Course Cycliste de Solidarnosc et des Champions Olympiques (2.2) was flat again, but the wind made it more selective and split the peloton. The front group kept changing, but yellow jersey Marceli Boguslawski was always missing until his teammates brought things back together for a bunch sprint. After another perfectly executed leadout, Marceli Boguslawski delivered the team’s third straight win.

item.title: Evenepoel: 'We both want to be on the podium with Tadej and Jonas'
item.description: Remco Evenepoel was not pleased with questions at the Tour de France press conference about a possible rivalry with Florian Lipowitz. The Belgian faces considerable pressure ahead of the start of the Tour, where his chances of still contending for the overall classification in a three-week race could become clear.

item.title: Bold Tour de France prediction on Kop over Kop: Isaac del Toro will win more stages than Tadej Pogacar
item.description: The Tour de France starts on Saturday, with all eyes naturally on Tadej Pogacar. But in Isaac del Toro, he has a rival in his own team.

item.title: Seixas and Pogacar attract watch deals just before the Tour de France
item.description: With the Tour de France about to begin, Tadej Pogacar and Paul Seixas are among the biggest names in cycling, and major companies are eager to sign them to sponsorship deals. In the watch industry, there was just enough time to secure agreements.

item.title: Tour queen stage under threat after mudslides cause road collapse
item.description: The Tour de France has not yet started, but organisers may already need to alter the route of the queen stage to Alpe d'Huez. Extreme weather over recent weeks has caused the asphalt on the Col de Sarenne to crumble twice.

item.title: Óscar Pereiro tests his memory of 2006, 20 years after his Tour win
item.description: Twenty years after the Tour that changed his life, Óscar Pereiro looks back at 2006 to see how much he remembers from that unforgettable year. From Barcelona’s Champions League title and Italy’s World Cup win to Fabio Cannavaro’s Ballon d’Or, Spain’s basketball world title, and the seasons of Nadal, Federer, Alonso and the Miami Heat, the Galician takes on MARCA’s quiz with memories, doubts and a few amusing mistakes. He even gets one cycling answer wrong, thinking Valverde won that year when it was Vinokourov.

item.title: Óscar Pereiro takes on a Tour de France mountain pass quiz 17 years after retirement
item.description: The 2026 Tour de France will climb some of the most iconic passes in the history of the Grande Boucle. Can 2006 winner Óscar Pereiro still recognize them?

item.title: Michael Boogerd explains why this Tour will still be exciting despite Tadej Pogacar
item.description: Michael Boogerd will work the full Tour de France this year as a TV commentator for NOS. He will form the regular live commentary duo on Dutch television with Andries Lamain and will also appear five times on De Avondetappe. In an interview, Boogerd explains why he is still looking forward to an exciting Tour despite Tadej Pogacar’s presence.

item.title: Tour 2026: More Belgians than French riders at the start this year
item.description: In the early years of the Tour de France, the peloton was made up almost entirely of French riders, but that is no longer the case. This year, there will even be more Belgians than French riders at the start of La Grande Boucle, an unusual situation but not the first time in Tour history.

item.title: Van der Poel says he loves and hates the Tour before the Grand Départ in Barcelona
item.description: Mathieu van der Poel is one of the best Classics riders of all time, but his relationship with Grand Tours is more complicated. He has ridden the Tour de France five times, won two stages and worn the yellow jersey in two editions, but finished it only twice. He now says he appreciates the Tour more as he gets older and is considering ending his racing career after 2028.

item.title: Netcompany Ineos target Tour de France stages with strong opening team time trial lineup
item.description: Netcompany Ineos are heading to the Tour de France focused mainly on stage wins, with the opening team time trial offering their first major opportunity. The British team brings a powerful time trial squad to Barcelona, including Filippo Ganna, Joshua Tarling, Tobias Foss, Thymen Arensman, Dorian Godon, Kévin Vauquelin, Egan Bernal and Michał Kwiatkowski.

item.title: Marceli Boguslawski wins both opening half-stages of Course Cycliste de Solidarnosc et des Champions Olympiques
item.description: Course Cycliste de Solidarnosc et des Champions Olympiques (2.2) opened with two half-stages. Both ended in bunch sprints, and both were won by ATT Investments sprinter Marceli Boguslawski after strong team performances. With three days still to race, the Polish rider leads the yellow and white jersey classifications, while the Czech team tops the team standings. The two wins raise ATT Investments' season total in UCI races to nine and add 16 more UCI points.

item.title: Lidl-Trek balance Juan Ayuso's podium bid with Mads Pedersen's green jersey goal at Tour de France
item.description: Lidl-Trek head into this year's Tour de France with major ambitions on several fronts. The team wants to guide Juan Ayuso to the podium in Paris, while also chasing stage wins and the points classification with Mads Pedersen. The question is whether both goals can work within the same team.

item.title: Uno-X to replace 'icon' Cort for the Tour de France
item.description: Uno-X will replace 'icon' Cort in its Tour de France plans.

item.description: Tadej Pogacar, already often seen wearing a Richard Mille watch, is now also an ambassador for the brand. The Slovenian is fronting a new collaboration between Richard Mille and Colnago, the bike supplier of UAE Emirates XRG.

item.description: Mathieu van der Poel says he has started thinking about retirement and that the end of 2028 could be the right time to stop, though he stressed it is not a final decision. In the short term, he plans to finish the Tour de France and then prepare for the MTB World Championships in Val di Sole 2026.

item.title: Human rights groups call on UCI to suspend UAE Team Emirates-XRG licence
item.description: Human rights groups have called on the UCI to suspend the licence of UAE Team Emirates-XRG.

item.title: Another crash-filled Tour de France?
item.description: Crashes continue to shape professional cycling. Despite new UCI rules, safety at the Tour de France remains a central concern for riders and teams.

item.description: Mathieu van der Poel says his retirement could come in the near future, with 2028 a possible end point to his career.

item.title: Brian Holm assesses the balance of power between Team Visma | Lease a Bike and UAE Team Emirates - XRG at the Tour de France
item.description: Brian Holm says the team time trial could be a key factor at the Tour de France. He gives a slight edge to Team Visma | Lease a Bike, while also noting the strength of Pogacar and UAE Team Emirates - XRG.

item.title: A year ago: Patrick Lefevere criticised Ethan Hayter’s positioning at Soudal Quick-Step
item.description: In a look back one year, former Belgian manager Patrick Lefevere said seeing Ethan Hayter constantly at the back of the bunch gave him chills.

item.description: It is the largest donation ever made by his foundation.

item.title: Tadej Pogacar donates €100,000 to storm-hit hometown
item.description: Tadej Pogacar, currently preparing for the Tour de France, has donated €100,000 to his hometown of Komenda after it was hit by a severe storm earlier this month.

item.title: Tadej Pogacar donates €100,000 to storm-hit hometown
item.description: Tadej Pogacar, currently preparing for the Tour de France, has donated €100,000 to his hometown of Komenda after it was hit by a severe storm earlier this month.

item.title: Adrie van der Poel on Mathieu’s Tour de France: ‘If Van Aert can do that, Mathieu should be able to as well’
item.description: The Van der Poel family is looking ahead to a new Tour de France with excitement and respect. Mathieu van der Poel hopes to make a big impact again, but according to his father Adrie he will need to be in absolute top form.

item.title: Mathieu van der Poel and the possibility he could retire from cycling in 2.5 years
item.description: Mathieu van der Poel, now 31 and heading to the Tour de France as Alpecin-Premier Tech leader, is still at his peak. But the prospect that he could stop racing in as little as two and a half years is a reminder to appreciate him while he is still competing.

item.title: Pogacar aims to join Anquetil, Merckx, Hinault and Indurain among the five-time Tour de France winners
item.description: Tadej Pogacar is targeting a place in the exclusive club of five-time Tour de France champions. The article also looks at the full roll of honour and all past winners of the race.

item.title: Miguel Indurain shares his key names and predictions for the 2026 Tour de France
item.description: Miguel Indurain spoke to Eurosport ahead of this week’s Tour de France, discussing Tadej Pogacar, Juan Ayuso and Seixas. He said Pogacar is the clear favourite, but not without rivals.

item.title: Lenny Martinez targets a Tour de France stage win or polka-dot jersey
item.description: After a strong and consistent start to the season, Lenny Martinez of Bahrain Victorious will have freedom at the Tour de France to chase a stage victory or the mountains classification after two difficult editions in 2024 and 2025.

item.title: Van der Poel could still be a golden pick for your Tour de France fantasy team despite Pogacar and Philipsen
item.description: Mathieu van der Poel could offer major value for Tour de France fantasy players. The Alpecin-Premier Tech rider starts the race on 4 July and remains an appealing option even alongside stars such as Tadej Pogacar and Jasper Philipsen.

item.title: Van der Poel heads to the Tour without pressure
item.description: The Dutch star is taking the expectations around the Tour in stride.

item.description: Why are no cyclists testing positive for the use of cortisone?

item.title: Road cycling beginners: Tips for riding in the slipstream
item.description: Drafting is one of the most effective techniques for saving energy on a road bike, but it can feel unfamiliar for beginners. Here are some tips.

item.description: A guide to the best winning strategy for your free team in the Tour manager game.

item.title: Expert doubts Jonas: 'Last year changed everything'
item.description: Van Garderen says all the questions were answered there.

item.title: UAE Belgian ready to dominate with Pogacar in the Tour de France: 'The hardest fitness session of my life'
item.description: At UAE Emirates-XRG, everyone is ready to go all in with Tadej Pogacar in the Tour de France. The Slovenian has been outstanding all year and is expected to be even better in July, backed by seven top-level teammates including Tim Wellens.

item.title: Tour 2026: Belgium has never had so much sprinting depth
item.description: Fans hoping to see Belgian success in the Tour de France should mark the flat stages. Belgium has an exceptional group of fast riders, with five sprint opportunities on paper in this Tour de France.

item.title: Analysis: Paul Seixas is good for the Tour de France – but is the Tour de France good for him?
item.description: An analysis of whether Paul Seixas benefits the Tour de France, and whether racing the Tour de France is the right move for him.

item.title: Leaving Visma has not yet brought Valter success, but it has given him insight into Tour de France leaders Martinez and Tiberi
item.description: Attila Valter hoped his move from Visma | Lease a Bike to Bahrain Victorious would let him race for himself again, but the first half of the season has not gone to plan. The 28-year-old Hungarian will miss the Tour de France, though he has gained insight into team leaders Martinez and Tiberi.

item.title: Unconventional but effective: NSN's Plan B for Biniam Girmay, including his sprint train, for the Tour de France
item.description: Biniam Girmay surprisingly won three stages and the green jersey in the Tour de France two years ago. After an 18-month dry spell, the Eritrean has shown at NSN that he is back on track. What can be expected from him and his team in this Tour de France?

item.title: Tim van Dijke overcomes extra setback on road to Tour de France debut
item.description: Saturday 4 July will mark a new milestone in Tim van Dijke’s career. After the Giro d'Italia and Vuelta a Espana, he will make his Tour de France debut, following brother Mick. He is set to ride in support of Remco Evenepoel after earning the team’s full confidence in just a few months.

item.title: Milan Fretin gets full lead-out support for Tour de France
item.description: Jasper Philipsen (Alpecin-Premier Tech) and Tim Merlier (Soudal Quick-Step) are among the strongest sprinters in the Tour de France, but Belgian fast man Milan Fretin (Cofidis) is also targeting success. After knocking on the door for several years, Fretin now has his team’s full backing and will be supported by a complete lead-out train.

item.title: Bert Van Lerberghe on Tour de France sprint chances and the attacker Soudal Quick-Step wants to control
item.description: The Tour de France 2026 starts on Saturday in Barcelona, but the sprinters may have to wait until stage five for their first real opportunity. With a demanding opening weekend in Catalonia and the transfer into France via the Pyrenees, riders like Tim Merlier will first need to get through the early kilometers. Lead-out man Bert Van Lerberghe discussed the limited sprint chances and the attacking rider Soudal Quick-Step does not want to let go.

item.title: Lars Craps goes from long rehabilitation to Tour de France debut
item.description: Lars Craps earned a Tour de France selection after a very consistent Tour Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, riding in the shadow of Wout van Aert, Maxim Van Gils and Cian Uijtdebroeks. In Saint-Amand-Montrond he finished eighth from the breakaway, and in Crest-Voland he placed 13th after a long day out front.

item.title: Asgreen and Vandborg agree on what will decide the Tour team time trial
item.description: Constant speed, perfect turns and a united team. Brian Vandborg and Kasper Asgreen explain what it takes to win the opening stage team time trial of the Tour.

item.title: White jersey game: who can challenge Paul Seixas? Evenepoel is no longer eligible
item.description: The white jersey competition remains open, with Paul Seixas among the leading names. As a reminder, the rider wearing the white jersey earns 5 points for each day he finishes in it, while Remco Evenepoel is no longer eligible for the classification.

item.title: Tour 2026 preview: favorites for the mountains classification
item.description: Predicting the winner of the mountains classification is difficult, but that only makes it more interesting. General classification contenders can target the iconic polka-dot jersey, while attacking riders will also hope to take it. WielerFlits looks ahead at the main favorites.

item.description: According to early bookmakers' odds for Tour de France 2026, Pogačar starts as the clear favourite ahead of Vingegaard. Seixas is rated ahead of Del Toro and Evenepoel in the battle for the podium.

item.title: ‘It feels like a relief when Pogacar attacks’: French rider highlights his dominance
item.description: Tadej Pogacar is again the main favorite for the Tour de France ahead of Jonas Vingegaard and Paul Seixas. French rider Jordan Jegat, 10th overall last year, described how overwhelming Pogacar’s dominance can feel for other GC contenders.

item.title: ‘It feels like a scam when Pogacar attacks’: French rider paints stark picture of his dominance
item.description: Tadej Pogacar is again the main favorite for the Tour de France ahead of Jonas Vingegaard and Paul Seixas. French rider Jordan Jegat, 10th overall last year, described how daunting Pogacar’s dominance can feel for other GC contenders.

item.description: Zach Nehr examines the less obvious dangers of heat training and explains how cyclists can use it safely to gain performance benefits.

item.title: Tour de France: Florian Lipowitz targets more in his second appearance alongside Remco Evenepoel
item.description: After his sensational podium finish last year, Florian Lipowitz returns to the Tour de France. Signs suggest he could be even stronger this time, forming a two-pronged leadership with Olympic champion Remco Evenepoel.

item.title: Zipp’s new wheels reportedly faster than Scope at a much lower price
item.description: Zipp has unveiled a bold new budget-oriented wheelset aimed at a new segment of the market, with claims it is faster than Scope while costing far less.

item.description: Zipp has introduced the 404 S, a relatively affordable all-round road wheelset with a 50mm rim profile.

item.description: The Mexican is the in-form stage racer of 2026, although Tadej Pogačar stands in his way.

item.description: The debate surrounding Trine Vingegaard’s controversial interview last year has flared up again.

item.description: A week later than in most other countries, Sweden held its national time trial championships on Wednesday afternoon. The best-known names took the titles, with Lidl-Trek talent Jakob Söderqvist winning the men’s professional race.

item.title: Tour de France 2026 prize money unchanged at €2.5 million
item.description: The Tour de France 2026 prize fund remains virtually unchanged at €2.5 million, including €500,000 for the Yellow Jersey winner and €11,000 for each stage victory.

item.description: Several human rights organizations are calling for the immediate suspension of UAE Emirates-XRG, the team of Tadej Pogacar among others, over its links to the conflict in Sudan.

item.description: Remco Evenepoel will start his first Tour de France on Saturday with Red Bull-BORA-hansgrohe. These seven teammates are expected to help him target a podium finish in Paris.

item.title: Tour 2026: Five riders who could score mountain points
item.description: The mountains classification is often the hardest contest to predict in a Grand Tour, and the Tour de France 2026 is no different. Still, several riders have said they will target the polka-dot jersey, with two more added based on rider values.

item.description: Visma-Lease a Bike has made a significant investment ahead of the Tour de France, which could benefit Jonas Vingegaard.

item.description: The American brand says its new S-Works Tarmac SL9 is the fastest road bike it has ever made, using Formula 1 lap-time software to measure performance beyond aerodynamics and weight.

item.title: Valentin Madouas on missing Tour de France selection: "I deserved to be at the start"
item.description: Valentin Madouas said he deserved to be selected for the Tour de France after being left out of the lineup.

item.title: Valentin Madouas on Tour de France omission: "Disgusted... I don't understand"
item.description: Valentin Madouas said he was disgusted and did not understand why he was not selected for the Tour de France.

item.description: Valentin Madouas said he felt he deserved a place in the Tour de France squad, but was not selected.

item.title: Decathlon invests in Brompton to expand the folding bike brand
item.description: Decathlon has made a major investment in Brompton as the two companies aim to grow the future of the iconic folding bike, including expected 'Brompton corners' in Decathlon stores.

item.title: Overview: Only two Tour teams still to announce their squads
item.description: A full overview of the riders selected for the Tour, with only two teams yet to confirm their line-ups.

item.title: Specialized S-Works Tarmac SL9 review
item.description: The Specialized S-Works Tarmac was seen as a finely tuned race bike after the Venge aero model was discontinued. The new SL9 aims to raise the bar with aerodynamic improvements, and this test examines whether it succeeds.

item.title: LOOK 795 BladeRS 3 launched with 890g frame
item.description: The third-generation LOOK 795 Blade RS is lighter, more aerodynamic and offers more fit options, including stem, handlebar and seatpost offset choices. Prices start at €8,490.

item.title: Look says new race bike combines aero gains with real-world performance
item.description: Look says its latest race bike delivers a 15-watt saving over its predecessor while balancing aerodynamic efficiency with real-world ride performance.

item.title: Coppa Italia delle Regioni 2026: Tommaso Dati takes overall lead after Italian Championships
item.description: Tommaso Dati is the new leader of the Coppa Italia delle Regioni 2026. After finishing second in the road race at the Italian Championships, the Team UKYO rider moved to the top of the individual standings with 180 points, ahead of Giulio Pellizzari on 164. Davide Donati is third on 110, followed by Diego Ulissi on 109 and Federico Iacomoni on 104.

item.title: Gianni Vermeersch finished Belgian Championships with a broken rib
item.description: Gianni Vermeersch finished seventh at the Belgian national championships despite crashing a few laps earlier. A hospital visit in Torhout the day after the race in Brasschaat revealed he had suffered a broken rib.

item.title: Gianni Vermeersch finished Belgian Championships with a broken rib
item.description: Gianni Vermeersch finished seventh at the Belgian national championships despite crashing a few laps earlier. A hospital visit in Torhout the day after the race in Brasschaat revealed he had suffered a broken rib.

item.title: Juan Ayuso targets Tour de France podium as Lidl-Trek manage leadership options
item.description: Juan Ayuso said anything can change on any day in the Tour de France as he set a podium finish as his goal. Lidl-Trek are also targeting the points jersey with Mads Pedersen in the 2026 Tour.

item.title: National coach welcomes new Danish women’s team
item.description: A new women’s team, Team Cranks, is set to enter Danish cycling with the aim of creating Denmark’s strongest development environment.

item.title: Magnus Cort Nielsen and Amalie Dideriksen headline Danish championship weekend
item.description: Denmark crowned national champions across road, time trial, mountain bike and trial disciplines over the weekend. On the road, Magnus Cort Nielsen won the elite men’s road race and Amalie Dideriksen took the elite women’s title. In the time trials, Kristian Egholm won the elite men’s crown and Sif Nikoline Bendix Madsen claimed the elite women’s title. In mountain bike, Sofie Heby Pedersen and Rose-Mai Damsgaard each won two national titles in XCC and XCO, while Gustav Heby Pedersen won the elite men’s XCO title and Sebastian Fini Juul took XCC gold.

item.title: Lee Gibbons appointed Interim CEO of British Cycling
item.description: Lee Gibbons has been named Interim CEO of British Cycling after current CEO Jon Dutton left the role to join the British Olympic Association.

item.title: Tour de France coverage index
item.description: This item appears to be a list of Tour de France headlines rather than a single news story, including features on key stages, team guides, prize money, favourites, and reports on Edoardo Affini and a mudslide on the Alpe d’Huez stage route.

item.title: USA Cycling names squad for 2026 UCI BMX Racing World Championships
item.description: Fourteen American riders will represent the United States at the 2026 UCI BMX Racing World Championships in Brisbane, Australia, on July 18-19.

item.title: Is Arnaud De Lie poorly supported by Lotto-Intermarché at the Tour?
item.description: Questions have been raised about Arnaud De Lie’s support within Lotto-Intermarché for the Tour, with concerns that tensions in the team could affect its atmosphere.

item.title: Visma | Lease a Bike’s winter transfer strategy looks vindicated
item.description: Visma | Lease a Bike faced criticism after losing several big names without making equally high-profile signings, but by mid-2026 that view appears to be fading. Head of Strategy Patrick Broe says the team’s long-term vision is now also reflected in its Tour de France selection.

item.title: KNWU offers Digital Marketing & AI internship
item.description: Koninklijke Nederlandsche Wielren Unie is recruiting an intern for Digital Marketing & AI to support campaigns, content and the federation’s digital growth.

item.title: Pavel Bittner ready for Tour de France as Picnic PostNL’s main sprint option
item.description: Pavel Bittner has recovered from an ankle injury in time to return for this year’s Tour de France. The Czech sprinter will be Picnic PostNL’s main weapon as the Dutch team targets stage results at the race.

item.title: Adel wins Drásal and Czech MTB ultramarathon title
item.description: Adel won the Drásal race and became Czech national champion in MTB ultramarathon. The 33rd edition of the event also hosted the national championship, with riders tackling 165 kilometres and 4,447 metres of climbing in the Hostýn Hills in extreme heat.

item.title: Why Alberto Monti faded at the national championships, and why ATT Investments were not celebrating on the podium
item.description: Alberto Monti won the men's elite time trial, the only senior men's national title claimed by a Czech team. In the road race, he was the only rider to go with the early favorites from foreign teams, but after halfway he began to drop back and finished last among those who completed the race. Riders from ATT Investments reached the finish at the very front, yet had little reason to celebrate. Three podium places from the two elite races were no substitute for a single national title. Their consolation is ATT Investments' position in the UCI Europe Tour ranking, where they sit second.

item.title: Milan, Grégoire and Herregodts take major national titles as Kelderman also celebrates
item.description: National championships were also held across the rest of Europe over the weekend. UAE Emirates-XRG enjoyed a particularly successful weekend with titles in Italy, Switzerland and Portugal, while Lidl-Trek triumphed in the USA, Italy and the Czech Republic.

item.title: Tour de France debutants Paul Seixas and Olav Kooij to share Decathlon CMA CGM support as Gall misses out
item.description: Decathlon CMA CGM will not rely only on rising star Paul Seixas at this year's Tour de France. Sprinter Olav Kooij will also make his debut in the race after joining the French team with the Tour in mind. The question is whether the rest of the Decathlon lineup can support both ambitious riders.

item.title: Michael Kukrle was the top performer from the domestic conti scene at the national championships
item.description: At the national championships, Mathias Vacek again showed he is currently the strongest Czech rider, while the other elite podium finishers also underlined their quality. Looking beyond that top level, Michael Kukrle was the best rider from the Czech continental scene. He finished fifth in both the time trial and the road race. Those results reflect his strong season so far, which has made him the highest-ranked Czech rider at conti level in the UCI rankings.

item.description: The latest news from women's cycling, interviews with young riders and champions, in-depth features, technical highlights, and more.

item.description: Pinarello-Q36.5 will use a new race kit and a revised bike livery for the Tour de France 2026. The Swiss-licensed Professional team, which is set to include Tom Pidcock, will appear with a much lighter jersey made by primary sponsor Q36.5 and matching updated bikes.

item.title: Tour de France 2026 stages: full route from Barcelona to Paris
item.description: An overview of all stages of the 2026 Tour de France, from the start in Barcelona to the finish in Paris. The 113th edition includes several highlights, including eight mountain stages.

item.title: Visma | Lease a Bike cleared as Edoardo Affini set to start Tour de France
item.description: Edoardo Affini will be able to start the Tour de France on 4 July, Visma | Lease a Bike confirmed on Tuesday morning. The Dutch team had been waiting for an update on his condition after he crashed heavily in the Italian national time trial championships.

item.description: The Dutch squad has named a Tour de France team built to support stage-hunting leaders Jasper Philipsen and Mathieu van der Poel.

item.description: The Italian Professional team collected valuable UCI points in Uzbekistan with a one-two finish in the national time trial through Nikita Tsvetkov and Sergey Rostovsev, points that could matter in the fight to finish the season among the top 30 teams.

item.title: Riis surprised by selections from both Visma and UAE
item.description: Bjarne Riis called UAE’s Tour squad "disgusting," but said there is one omission he does not understand.

item.title: Visma | Lease a Bike expands focus on riders’ technical skills
item.description: Visma | Lease a Bike says it is taking another step in rider development by strengthening technical support across its teams. The squad is expanding its collaboration with bike handling specialist Maikel Govaarts.

item.title: Anders Lund sees points classification chances for Mads Pedersen despite Tour de France change
item.description: Anders Lund believes Mads Pedersen can still challenge for the Tour de France points classification despite changes to the competition, saying stage wins will now be even more important in the battle for the green jersey.

item.description: Lund believes Mads P. still has opportunities in the points classification despite recent changes, and points to the stages where he needs to deliver.

item.description: Juan Ayuso will try to secure Lidl-Trek’s first Tour de France podium since Richie Porte in 2020, while the team has clarified the roles of Ayuso and Skjelmose.

item.description: A request has been made to the UCI for the immediate suspension of UAE Emirates XRG, the team of Tadej Pogačar, just days before the Tour de France.

item.description: RAI has outlined its full Tour de France 2026 coverage, with no full live stage broadcasts but extensive programming from afternoon into the evening.

item.description: Edoardo Affini has been passed fit to start the Tour de France, bringing relief for Jonas Vingegaard and Visma.

item.description: The Slovenian is unlikely to wear the expensive Richard Mille watch in public during the Tour de France because of safety concerns.

item.description: Cian Uijtdebroeks now knows his seven Movistar teammates for the Tour de France, with Einer Rubio and Pablo Castrillo among those set to support the Belgian GC rider.

item.description: Soudal Quick-Step has named its eight-man squad for the 2026 Tour de France. With Remco Evenepoel no longer on the team, the Belgian squad will focus on stage sprints, the points classification, the mountains classification and the general classification.

item.description: Soudal Quick-Step has announced its Tour de France lineup, featuring Tim Merlier and Aurélien Paret-Peintre but not Mikel Landa.

item.title: Cian Uijtdebroeks to lead Movistar at the Tour de France without Enric Mas
item.description: The Belgian will be Movistar’s main leader for the Tour de France, with Enric Mas absent from the Spanish team. Mikel Landa will also not be there with Soudal Quick-Step.

item.title: Controversy over new book by Ruben Van Gucht
item.description: Ruben Van Gucht has sparked controversy with his new book, which includes the initials of 70 former bed partners in 'De man, het raadsel, het quizboek'.

item.title: Ruben Van Gucht publishes initials of 70 bed partners in new book
item.description: In his new quiz book, Ruben Van Gucht includes a list of the initials of 70 bed partners as a reference to his private life.

item.description: Ruben Van Gucht has made public the list of his bed partners in his new quiz book, 'De man, het raadsel, het quizboek', by publishing their initials.

item.title: Soudal Quick-Step target stage wins at the Tour de France with Tim Merlier and Valentin Paret-Peintre
item.description: Soudal Quick-Step head from Catalonia to Paris with two main goals: to win at least one stage and to deliver a strong Tour de France with riders including Tim Merlier and Valentin Paret-Peintre.

item.title: Cian Uijtdebroeks backed by strong climbing and time trial support for Tour de France with Movistar
item.description: After leaving Visma | Lease a Bike, Cian Uijtdebroeks gets an immediate chance to lead Movistar at the Tour de France. The Spanish team will support the 23-year-old Belgian with strong climbers and powerful riders for the team time trial on day 1.

item.title: Tension grows at Groupama-FDJ United after Valentin Madouas is left out of the Tour de France squad
item.description: Groupama-FDJ United have faced more internal tension before the Tour de France, where the French team will field a young, attacking lineup without David Gaudu. The atmosphere worsened further after Valentin Madouas was also left out.

item.title: Tim Merlier leads Soudal Quick-Step’s hunt for Tour de France stage wins, with Mikel Landa absent and Ilan Van Wilder targeting the GC
item.description: Soudal Quick-Step will focus primarily on stage wins at the Tour de France. With Remco Evenepoel gone, the Belgian team returns to a sprint-centered lineup built around Tim Merlier, supported by Pascal Eenkhoorn, Dylan van Baarle, Bert Van Lerberghe and Jasper Stuyven. Mikel Landa is absent, while Ilan Van Wilder will aim for the general classification.

item.title: Cian Uijtdebroeks to make Tour de France debut as Movistar leader
item.description: After six seasons with Enric Mas leading the team, Movistar have changed direction this year by naming Cian Uijtdebroeks as their leader for his first Tour de France.

item.title: Tour de France 2026: Movistar brings multiple options around Cian Uijtdebroeks
item.description: Movistar will have several options at the Tour de France 2026, but the main focus is new signing Cian Uijtdebroeks. The Belgian, who joined the Spanish team this winter after leaving Visma | Lease a Bike, is set to lead the general classification challenge. He will be supported by a squad built to help him in the mountains and ease some of the pressure of leadership.

item.title: Tour 2026: Soudal Quick-Step leaves Mikel Landa out, includes five compatriots
item.description: Soudal Quick-Step has submitted its squad for the Tour de France. As previously reported, Mikel Landa is not part of the eight-man selection. Pascal Eenkhoorn and Dylan van Baarle have been included for the three-week race.

item.title: Tour 2026: Soudal Quick-Step leaves Mikel Landa out, while Eenkhoorn and Van Baarle are selected
item.description: Soudal Quick-Step has confirmed its squad for the Tour de France. Mikel Landa has been left out of the eight-man selection, with the team deciding not to take the struggling Basque rider. Pascal Eenkhoorn and Dylan van Baarle are included.

item.title: 'Not worth spending a penny on' - DCU responds to Kyllingen's World Championships proposal
item.description: Michael Rasmussen says DCU should spend its World Championships budget on young, medal-hungry male riders rather than the Danish elite women.

item.title: Movistar Team confirms its Tour eight with new leader in the absence of Enric Mas: Uijtdebroeks ready to target the general classification
item.description: Movistar Team has confirmed its lineup for the Tour de France 2026, with Belgian rider Cian Uijtdebroecks set to lead the team in the fight for a strong general classification result in the absence of Enric Mas.

item.title: Pinarello-Q36.5 launches limited-edition Tour de France kit as Tom Pidcock returns
item.description: The team has unveiled a special-edition kit for the Tour de France, aiming for a cooler and more distinctive visual identity, as team leader Tom Pidcock returns to racing.

item.title: Dati: Italian silver, comparison with the greats, and a future still to be written
item.description: Tommaso Dati's runner-up finish at the Italian championships confirms his progress and growing confidence. He is now looking ahead to the future, but without rushing.

item.title: Organizations call on UCI to suspend UAE Emirates over war in Sudan
item.description: A coalition of human rights organizations, including FairSquare, has asked the UCI to immediately suspend UAE Emirates XRG over what it describes as the United Arab Emirates' support for forces committing genocide in Sudan.

item.title: Human rights groups call on UCI to suspend UAE Emirates XRG over war in Sudan
item.description: A coalition of human rights organisations, including FairSquare, has asked the UCI to immediately suspend UAE Emirates XRG over what it says is the United Arab Emirates' support for forces committing genocide in Sudan.

item.title: Tadej Pogacar Foundation donates €100,000 after storm hits hometown of Komenda
item.description: The Tadej Pogacar Foundation has donated €100,000 after a devastating storm struck Pogacar's hometown of Komenda.

item.title: How Anquetil, Merckx, Hinault and Indurain won their fifth Tour de France

item.title: Paula Blasi reportedly set to join Movistar from 2027
item.description: One of the biggest transfers in recent years in the women’s peloton could be nearing completion, with multiple foreign media reports saying Paula Blasi will ride for Movistar from 2027. There has been no official confirmation yet.

item.title: Jakub Otruba heads to his first Tour de France. He will help Gaviria in the sprints and get a chance in breakaways
item.description: Czech rider Jakub Otruba will line up for the Tour de France for the first time in his career. The Caja Rural-Seguros RGA rider made his Grand Tour debut at last year’s Vuelta a España, where he featured several times in long breakaways. He is expected to target similar moves in France.

item.title: Championships full of surprises and familiar names on top. Lotte Kopecky falls in Belgium, Elisa Longo Borghini wins in Italy
item.description: The women’s national championships produced several big stories over the weekend. Lotte Kopecky was beaten by Shari Bossuyt in Belgium, Lieke Nooijen won the Dutch title, and Elisa Longo Borghini confirmed her favorite status in Italy. Julia Kopecký will now wear the Czech champion’s jersey, while Viktória Chladoňová retained the Slovak title.

item.title: Pogačar already knows who will help him win a fifth Tour de France title
item.description: Tadej Pogačar is the clear favorite for this year’s Tour de France, but he will not win a fifth title without a strong team. UAE Emirates-XRG has named seven domestiques for this year, including Isaac del Toro and Adam Yates. Pavel Sivakov did not make the lineup.

item.title: Magnus Cort announces retirement, then wins first Danish title ahead of Tour de France
item.description: Magnus Cort has had a remarkable week. After being selected by Uno-X Mobility for the Tour de France, getting engaged, and announcing he will retire after this season, the 33-year-old then won the Danish national title for the first time in his career.

The Antwerp rider finished fifth in the sprint against the professional women and was the best elite rider without a contract. A remarkable performance for the mother of five, who was pregnant with twins a year ago.

item.title: KNWU and JOIN join forces for recreational cyclists
item.description: The KNWU and JOIN are working closely together to better support recreational cyclists in the Netherlands with training, development, and sporting goals. Through this long-term partnership, the cycling federation is combining its knowledge, network, and expertise with one of the leading training platforms for cyclists.

item.title: Cyclists face more crashes ahead of the holidays, BESIP, Dám respekt and Wolt remind road users to be careful
item.description: With warmer weather and the holidays approaching, more cyclists and scooter riders are out on the streets and roads, and crashes involving them are also increasing. Representatives of BESIP, the Czech Ministry of Transport, the Dám respekt initiative and technology company Wolt highlighted the need to follow the rules, keep a safe distance and show greater consideration among all road users at Wednesday’s awareness event, Den respektu, on Prague’s Ludvík Svoboda embankment.

item.title: Mathias Vacek says it was very hard today as he wins a third national title
item.description: The Czech and Slovak Championships in Jeseník ended with the expected victories for Mathias Vacek and Lukáš Kubiš. All the main contenders agreed that Vacek was a deserved winner of an exceptionally demanding championship. “I have to say it was really hard today. Right from the start eight of us got away, and we took turns well in the group, so the pace was very high, which was quite demanding,” Vacek said after the finish.

item.title: Mathias Vacek wins his third Czech title in Jeseník as Lukáš Kubiš also celebrates
item.description: Mathias Vacek confirmed his status as the main favorite and comfortably defended his title in the elite men’s road race at the Czech Championships in Jeseník. The Lidl-Trek rider sealed victory with an attack on the final climb before the finish and claimed his third national title. Tomáš Přidal (United Shipping) took second, while Jakub Otruba (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA) finished third. Lukáš Kubiš (Unibet Rose Rockets) became Slovak champion for the third year in a row.

item.title: Live coverage of the men’s race at the Czech and Slovak Championships in Jeseník
item.description: Follow the action from the hot Czech and Slovak Championships in Jeseník with us. Organizers moved the start of the 208-kilometer race 30 minutes earlier, but extreme conditions and a brutally hard day in the saddle are still expected.

item.title: After Remco Evenepoel, Tim Wellens also withdraws from Belgian Championships
item.description: Defending champion Tim Wellens has also withdrawn from the Belgian road championships on Sunday. Wellens was still on the provisional start list on Saturday, but decided not to race. Remco Evenepoel had already used a medical certificate to avoid the ‘mandatory’ Belgian Championships. Wout van Aert will also not start and, due to the after-effects of his elbow injury, will not ride the Tour de France either.

item.title: Heat, melting asphalt and referee intervention: Jeseník championship faces safety concerns before men’s race
item.description: Extreme heat has significantly affected the Czech and Slovak national championships in Jeseník. The women’s road race was shortened because of surface problems on Rejvíz, and organizers are now deciding how Sunday’s men’s race will look. Race director Leopold König says safety is the top priority. Mathias Vacek and Lukáš Kubiš expect a hard and attractive race, but both admit the condition of the course could play an important role.

item.title: Julia Kopecký wins Czech road title after sprinting past Zemanová
item.description: Julia Kopecký became Czech road race champion in Jeseník. In the sun-baked race, which officials shortened to three laps because of surface problems on Rejvíz, she beat Kristýna Zemanová in the sprint. Barbora Němcová took bronze. Viktória Chladoňová won the Slovak title, finishing just ahead of the Czech pair in the overall standings. Among junior women, Karolína Špicarová defended her national title.

item.title: 22-year-old Kristian Egholm wins Danish time trial title in major upset
item.description: There were several surprises at the Danish time trial championships. In the women’s race, triathlete Sif Nikoline Bendix Madsen won with a time of 37:08.58. In the men’s race, 22-year-old Kristian Egholm of Lidl-Trek Future Racing caused the biggest upset, winning in 33:20.54. Men’s top 3: Kristian Egholm (Lidl-Trek Future Racing) 33:20.54, Mikkel Norsgaard Bjerg (UAE Team Emirates-XRG) +0:38, Peter Øxenberg Hansen (INEOS Grenadiers) +5:10. Women’s top 3: Sif Nikoline Bendix Madsen (Aalborg Cykle-Ring) 37:08.58, Gertrud Riis Madsen (Team Aalborg Sparekassen Danmark Women) +9:36, Silje Weitze Antvorskov (Team FRIIS) +1:05.39.
